source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When escape velocity is given to a particle on the surface of earth, its total energy is:
questions.explanation: At escape, total energy is zero (KE equals magnitude of PE).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The ratio of distances of two satellites from the centre of earth is 1:4. The ratio of their time periods is:
questions.explanation: T proportional to r^(3/2). So (1/4)^(3/2)=1/8.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a satellite in elliptical orbit, which quantity does NOT remain constant?
questions.explanation: Linear momentum changes in magnitude and direction in elliptical orbit.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An earth satellite is moved from one stable circular orbit to another higher circular orbit. Which quantity increases?
questions.explanation: At higher orbit, angular momentum increases while speed and angular velocity decrease.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two particles of masses m and M start from rest at infinite separation and attract each other. Their relative speed at separation d is:
questions.explanation: Using reduced mass: v_rel = sqrt(2G(M+m)/d).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Orbital velocity just above earth surface is v. At altitude h=R/2, orbital velocity is:
questions.explanation: v = sqrt(GM/r). With r=3R/2, v = v0*sqrt(2/3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Escape velocity vertically upward from earth surface is 11.2 km/s. If projected at 60 deg to the vertical, escape velocity is:
questions.explanation: Escape speed is independent of direction for a given point.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Inside a uniform spherical shell,
questions.explanation: Gravitational field inside a shell is zero and potential is constant (not zero in general).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An earth-orbiting satellite will escape if:
questions.optionB: its speed is made 1/sqrt(3) times its value
questions.optionD: it keeps moving in the orbit
questions.explanation: Escape speed is sqrt(2) times circular speed (about 41% increase).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite in circular orbit has total energy E0. Its potential energy is:
questions.explanation: For circular orbit, U = 2E (E is negative).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite in equatorial orbit has time period 6 h. It is overhead an equator observer; it will be overhead again after:
questions.explanation: Relative period: 1/Trel = |1/6 - 1/24| => Trel = 8 h.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A simple pendulum has period T1 on earth surface and T2 at height R (earth radius). The value of T2/T1 is:
questions.explanation: g at height R becomes g/4, so period doubles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: At what height does gravitational field reduce by 75% of surface value? (R is earth radius)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two masses m and M are separated by distance d. The gravitational potential at the point where net field is zero is:
questions.explanation: At neutral point r1:r2 = sqrt(M):sqrt(m), so V = -G( sqrt(M)+sqrt(m) )^2/d.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Gravitational potential energy of a body at distance r from earth center is U. Its weight at distance 2r is:
questions.explanation: U = -GMm/r, weight at 2r is GMm/(4r^2) = -U/(4r).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two satellites of equal mass in same orbit move opposite and collide inelastically, sticking together. Total energy just after collision is:
questions.explanation: They come to rest at radius r, so KE=0, PE = -G M (2m)/r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Weights of an object in coal mine, sea level, mountain top are w1, w2, w3 respectively. Then:
questions.explanation: Weight is maximum at sea level and decreases both at depth and height.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A thin rod of length L is bent into a semicircle of mass M. Gravitational potential at center is:
questions.explanation: All elements are at radius R = L/pi. So V = -GM/R = -GM*pi/L.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If V is gravitational potential on earth surface, its value at center of earth is:
questions.explanation: For uniform earth, V_center = -3GM/(2R) = 3V/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If g on planet A is 9 times that on planet B. A man jumps 2 m on planet A. Height of jump on planet B is:
questions.explanation: Jump height proportional to 1/g, so h_B = 9*h_A = 18 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body weighs 72 N at earth surface. At height h=R, its weight is:
questions.explanation: Weight reduces by factor (R/(R+R))^2 = 1/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass m is taken from earth surface to height h=3R. Change in gravitational potential energy is:
questions.explanation: Delta U = GMm(1/R - 1/4R) = 3GMm/4R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An artificial satellite moves in circular orbit. If its speed is half the escape velocity, its height above earth surface (radius R) is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A planet moves in an elliptical orbit with maximum and minimum distances r1 and r2 from sun. Its time period is proportional to:
questions.explanation: T proportional to a^(3/2), where a = (r1 + r2)/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite is in circular orbit of radius R. Another at 1.01R. The second period is longer by approximately:
questions.explanation: Delta T/T approx (3/2) * 1% = 1.5%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Earth rotates so that a body at latitude 60 deg is weightless. Duration of day would be:
questions.explanation: Condition: omega^2 R cos^2 60 = g. So omega = 2*sqrt(g/R), T=2*pi/omega=pi*sqrt(R/g).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the radius of earth's orbit is made one fourth, the duration of a year becomes:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An earth satellite S has orbit radius 4 times that of communication satellite C. The period of S is:
questions.explanation: T proportional r^(3/2). 4^(3/2)=8. If geo period is 1 day, S period is 8 days.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If potential energy of a body on a planet is U and escape velocity is v, then U equals:
questions.explanation: U = -GMm/R and v^2 = 2GM/R, so U = -1/2 m v^2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If change in g at height h above earth equals change at depth x below surface, then:
questions.explanation: g_h approx g(1-2h/R), g_d = g(1-d/R). Equating gives d=2h.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A tunnel is dug through earth diameter. A particle dropped from rest falls in. Choose the wrong statement:
questions.optionD: particle will drop to the center and stay
questions.explanation: It oscillates through the center; it does not stay there.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Four equal masses M at corners of square of side a. Escape velocity from center is:
questions.explanation: Potential at center V = -4GM/(a/sqrt2) = -4sqrt2 GM/a. v_e = sqrt(2|V|)=sqrt(8sqrt2 GM/a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Escape velocity from earth is v0. A planet has mass 3M and radius 3R. Escape velocity from planet is:
questions.explanation: v_e proportional sqrt(M/R). With M and R scaled by 3, ratio is 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite of mass m moving in circular orbit radius R has angular momentum L. Areal velocity is:
questions.explanation: Areal velocity = L/(2m).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If earth loses its gravity, for a body:
questions.optionA: weight becomes zero but mass does not
questions.optionB: mass becomes zero but weight does not
questions.optionC: both mass and weight become zero
questions.optionD: neither mass nor weight becomes zero
questions.explanation: Weight depends on g, mass does not.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Escape velocity from earth is 11.2 km/s. If a planet has radius R/2 and mass M/4, its escape velocity is:
questions.explanation: v_e proportional sqrt(M/R). Ratio sqrt((1/4)/(1/2))=1/sqrt(2). 11.2/sqrt(2) approx 8 km/s.
